fixedIssues: vision:broken_render:Reflect tab (02-tab-1-__Reflect.) Reflect and Settings tabs both render the paywall instead of their own screens
vision:contrast:02-tab-1-__Reflect.png Zero-value days in weekly bar chart are nearly invisible
notes: Fix 1: app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx's dismissModalsOnTabPress relied on a race between the default tabPress action (switching the active tab) and an imperative router.dismissAll() side effect — on web export this let the paywall modal (auto-pushed from Reflect's first-visit hard-paywall trigger) stay pinned on top no matter which tab was tapped underneath, so Reflect and Settings both appeared to show the paywall. Replaced with per-tab listeners that call router.dismissTo(href), which dismisses any modal and navigates to the target tab as a single atomic transition, eliminating the race. Fix 2: components/EmberStrip.tsx rendered zero-value days as a solid smoke-colored sliver, which is too close in luminance to the dark card background to read as a bar. Zero-value bars now render as an outlined (not filled) bar using theme.colors.textMuted, a color already tuned for legibility against the surface in both themes, plus a slightly taller minimum height (10px -> 14px) for clearer visibility. npx tsc --noEmit is clean.
